description_long: This is a futuristic, compact airplane with a sleek, angular fuselage and a predominantly dark gray exterior accented by orange and teal highlights. The wings are short and stubby, extending laterally from the midsection, and feature integrated engine pods with teal-colored intakes or lights at the front. The cockpit is centrally located and appears to be enclosed, with a faceted canopy that blends smoothly into the body. The rear of the airplane tapers to a point, with subtle orange accents and a low-profile tail assembly. The overall design suggests a high-tech, possibly urban or sci-fi utility aircraft, with a robust and armored appearance. No visible landing gear is present, and the surface finish is smooth and slightly reflective.
description: A compact, angular, dark gray airplane with orange and teal accents, featuring short mid-mounted wings and a sleek, enclosed cockpit. The design is futuristic and robust, with integrated engine pods and a low-profile tail.
description_view_0: Front view shows the angular nose, central cockpit canopy, and prominent teal-accented engine pods on either side of the fuselage.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the short, mid-mounted wing, integrated engine pod, and the smooth, tapered rear fuselage.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the pointed tail, low-profile tail assembly, and the symmetry of the wings and engine pods.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the wing, engine pod, and the streamlined body with orange accent details.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with short, straight wings featuring integrated engine pods and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines integrated into the wing roots with visible teal intakes.
attribute_tail_design: Low-profile conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and no prominent horizontal stabilizers visible.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a faceted canopy integrated into the fuselage, likely single-seat.
